data.title: Robert Half Inc. 1Q 2026: Revenue $1.3B, EPS $0.14— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Robert Half Inc. reported first-quarter 2026 results with service revenues of $1.3B and diluted EPS of $0.14, compared with $1.35B and $0.17 in the year-ago quarter, as net income fell to $13.79M from $17.35M.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$1.3B$1.35B(3.8%)
Net income²$13.79M$17.35M(20.5%)
Diluted EPS³$0.14$0.17(17.6%)

¹ Reported as “Service revenues”. ² Reported as “Net income”. ³ Reported as “income per share”.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend: Service revenues declined 3.8% year over year to $1.30B, with U.S. weakness offset in part by international growth.
  • Channel shift: Contract revenues fell with hours down about 7.2% while bill rates rose; permanent placement activity also declined.
  • Protiviti: The consulting segment remained relatively resilient with only a modest revenue decline, stronger international growth and higher adjusted margins.
  • Investment and operations: The company continued heavy investment in technology and AI, including significant software and cloud spending; working capital was driven by receivables and headcount was largely stable aside from reductions at Protiviti.

Original SEC Filing: ROBERT HALF INC. [ RHI ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Omega Flex, Inc. 1Q 2026: Revenue $23.09M, EPS $0.21— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Omega Flex, Inc. reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue of $23.09M and diluted EPS of $0.21, as net income attributable to the company fell to $2.08M versus the year-ago quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue was $23.09M for Q1 2026, down from $23.33M in Q1 2025 ( (1.0%)).
  • Net income attributable to Omega Flex, Inc. was $2.08M for Q1 2026, down from $3.57M in Q1 2025 ( (41.8%)).
  • Diluted earnings per share was $0.21 for Q1 2026, down from $0.35 in Q1 2025 ( (40.0%)).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend: Q1 sales modestly declined 1.0% year-over-year to $23.1M, indicating near-term flat demand in core markets.
  • Gross margin pressure: Gross margin fell to 56.7% from 60.3%, driven primarily by higher raw material costs and tariffs.
  • Channel & distribution: Majority of sales continued through independent reps, wholesalers and distributors across a broad North American network.
  • Product & brand momentum: The company continued to emphasize TracPipe® CSST, AutoFlare® fittings and newer MediTrac® CMT for faster installation benefits.
  • Operational investments: Increased engineering spend for product development and certification, and ongoing capital expenditures on manufacturing equipment.

Original SEC Filing: Omega Flex, Inc. [ OFLX ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: CROWN HOLDINGS, INC. 1Q 2026: Revenue $3.26B, EPS $1.56— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Crown Holdings, Inc. reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue rising to $3.26B while net income and diluted EPS declined versus the prior-year quarter, reflecting pass-through material cost increases, volume shifts across regions and margin pressure where costs were not fully recovered.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$3.26B$2.89B12.9%
Net income²$175M$193M(9.3%)
Diluted EPS³$1.56$1.65(5.5%)

¹ Reported as “Net sales”. ² Reported as “Net income attributable to Crown Holdings”. ³ Reported as “Earnings per common share attributable to Crown Holdings”.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth was driven by pass‑through material cost increases and volume gains.
  • Beverage can volumes rose in Europe and Asia Pacific, while Brazil volumes fell about 5%, weighing on Americas margins.
  • Continued progress on the Twentyby30 ESG program; company was recognized as a Forbes Net Zero Leader in 2025.
  • Operational capacity expansions include a new high‑speed line in Ponta Grossa, Brazil and planned capacity additions in Greece, Spain and India for 2026–2027.
  • Pricing and material cost pass‑throughs supported top-line growth, but margins were pressured where costs could not be fully recovered.

Original SEC Filing: CROWN HOLDINGS, INC. [ CCK ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Business First Bancshares 1Q 2026: Revenue $3.14M, Net income $22.21M, EPS $0.68— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Business First Bancshares reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue up modestly and earnings stronger year over year, reflecting loan growth, deposit gains and the impact of recent acquisition activity.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$3.14M$2.86M9.9%
Net income²$22.21M$19.19M15.7%
Diluted EPS³$0.68$0.654.6%

¹ Reported as “Service Charges on Deposit Accounts”. ² Reported as “Net Income Available to Common Shareholders”. ³ Reported as “Earnings Per Common Share”.

Business Highlights

  • Loan growth and core revenue: Total loans rose about 8% quarter over quarter, boosting net interest income and supporting revenue growth.
  • Deposit and funding mix shift: Deposits increased about 11%, with notable growth in noninterest-bearing accounts, helping to lower funding costs.
  • M&A expansion: Closed the Progressive acquisition on Jan. 1, 2026, adding scale, branches and a larger regional footprint in Texas and Louisiana.
  • Technology and efficiency: Announced a partnership with Covecta to deploy agentic AI aimed at streamlining deposit and loan workflows.
  • Asset quality oversight: The bank increased monitoring and reserves amid higher nonperforming loans, with targeted credit reviews and tighter oversight.

Original SEC Filing: Business First Bancshares, Inc. [ BFST ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Sandisk's Long-Term Contracts Raising Questions — Market Talk
data.description: Sandisk is likely to be able to exceed its 2Q guidance given strong pricing trends for memory technology, but the company's shift toward long-term supply agreements raises questions about whether it's taking full advantage ofpricing opportunities, Wedbush analysts write in a note. "Ultimately how one feels about the value of and ultimate sustainability of the new LTAs, vs. Sandisk's seemingly lost opportunity to push higher pricing right now, is going to dictate how one feels about SNDK's results," they write. "We aren't yet sure how this new set of LTAs will play out and likely will have to better understand the mechanics (details from our checks to date have been relatively sparse) to have a stronger opinion." Sandisk is up 5.1%. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

data.title: Cathie Wood's ARK Bought Alphabet, Meta — and Sold This Chip Stock — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni

Cathie Wood's ARK Invest doubled down on Big Tech this week, following through on its strategy of buying high-conviction names at any price.

Even though first-quarter earnings pushed shares higher, ARK bought Alphabet across three of its actively managed funds. The ARK Innovation exchange-traded fund (ticker: ARKK) snapped up 43,953 Class C shares on Thursday. As of Friday, ARKK held 250,688 Alphabet shares valued at roughly $95.7 million, or 1.48% of the portfolio by weight.

The ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ) purchased 28,180 Alphabet shares, while the ARK Space & Defense Innovation ETF (ARKX) added 11,996 shares. Alphabet stock comprises a larger portion of each of those funds, at 4.63% and 4.04%, respectively.

ARK Invest also bought the dip in shares of Meta Platforms after the parent company of Facebook shared its lofty capex plans. ARKK added 26,753 shares, the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF added 13,653 shares, and the ARK Blockchain & Fintech Innovation ETF added 6,795 shares. Meta comprises a relatively smaller part of those portfolios, ranging from 0.6% for ARKK to more than 2% for the others.

Also on Thursday, ARK Invest sold a combined 172,305 shares of Advanced Micro Devices across each of the five funds.

As of Thursday's close, more than half of the aforementioned funds had a negative annualized total return for the past five years. The biggest loser was the flagship ARKK fund, down 8.73% over the past five years. ARKQ and ARKX fared notably better, rising 9.25% and 9.89%, respectively, although their returns lagged behind the market. Both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite returned more than 13% over the same time period.

However, over the past three years, all five of ARK's actively managed funds have outperformed both indexes.

Inside Scoop is a regular Barron's feature covering stock transactions by corporate executives and board members — so-called insiders — as well as large shareholders, politicians, and other prominent figures. Due to their insider status, these investors are required to disclose stock trades with the Securities and Exchange Commission or other regulatory groups.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: HF Sinclair Expects West Coast Refinery Upgrades to Address Tight Fuel Market — OPIS
data.description:
Recently completed projects to boost production flexibility at HF Sinclair's 149,000 b/d Puget Sound refinery in Washington state should help address a tightening U.S. West Coast market due to the ongoing Middle East supply crisis, the company said Friday.

A project allowing the Puget Sound refinery to "swing" 7,000 b/d of its production between diesel and jet fuel depending on market conditions was brought online at the end of a Q4 2025 turnaround, Executive Vice President of Commercial Steven Ledbetter told analysts during a call.

"That's paying off greatly, not only to the West Coast, but also into markets in Latin America," Ledbetter said.

In addition, he said the Washington refinery also completed another flexibility project last year allowing it to produce and "swell" the gasoline pool in either making CARB-complied products or selling high-valued unfinished components like alkylate into California which has been more profitable.

Meanwhile, Ledbetter the company continued to evaluate a previously announced refined product pipeline expansion project to deliver fuels to the West Coast markets from its refineries in the Rockies.

HF Sinclair also completed a turnaround at its 41,400 b/d Woods Cross refinery in Utah during Q1, and the company does not have any planned maintenance scheduled until the back end of Q3 for its 165,000 b/d El Dorado refinery in Kansas.

Ledbetter also said it continued to advance a vacuum distillation project at El Dorado to improve product yield and allow an incremental 10,000 b/d of heavy crude into its feedstock mix. That project is expected to come online as part of El Dorado's fall turnaround this year.

HF Sinclair, which also supplies fuel to Sinclair branded gas stations, added 25 Sinclair-branded sites during Q1, with more than 100 sites with contracts signed and expected to come online over the next 6-12 months, Ledbetter said. The company still expects to grow the number of branded sites by about 10% annually, he said.

Since start of the Middle East conflict, HF Sinclair has not faced any crude oil supply challenges because of its access to multiple U.S. oil hubs and the ability to run different grades of crude, from Canadian heavy to Alaskan North Slope, Ledbetter said.

The company — which operates seven U.S. refineries and supplies fuels to more than 2,000 fuel sites — swung to a Q1 profit of $650 million from a loss of $2 million in the same quarter last year. During the call, Chief Executive Franklin Myers did not address the company's ongoing management issues after both its former CEO and chief financial officer took leaves of absences during Q1.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

  • Reporting by Frank Tang, ftang@opisnet.com; Editing by Steve Cronin, scronin@opisnet.com

data.title: Treasury Yields Have Another Rising Week Amid U.S.-Iran Standoff — Market Talk
data.description: Treasury yields rise in a week marked by robust U.S. indicators and dimming hopes of an end to tanker traffic obstruction in the Strait of Hormuz. Markets will focus on labor data next week. Fed officials express concerns over war-induced inflation. Citi analysts expect little energy inflation pass-through, leaving room for the Fed to react to a potential rise in unemployment. Citi expects the Fed to cut rates by 75 basis points this year. The 10-year yield rises 0.069 percentage point this week, to 4.377% and the two-year rises 0.112 point to 3.887%. Both are little changed on the day. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: Correction to How These New Funds Squeeze Article — WSJ
data.description: Matt Kaufman, head of ETFs at Calamos, says: "You're going to get paid 14% on a monthly basis as long as [the reference index tied to the S&P 500] doesn't go down 40% or more." "How These New Funds Squeeze 14% Yields Out of Stocks," at 10 a.m. ET, incorrectly quoted Kaufman saying that the fund's yield depends on the S&P 500.

data.title: Airlines Prepping for Potential Spirit Shutdown — WSJ
data.description:
By Jacob Passy

Competitors of Spirit Airlines are readying their game plans in the event the budget carrier ceases operations.

American Airlines has already begun putting caps on fares for economy-class tickets on nonstop routes that overlap with Spirit's network, a spokeswoman said. "The American Airlines team is prepared to do all it can to support Spirit Airlines customers andteam members," she said.

A United Airlines spokesperson said the company was preparing to support Spirit employees and customers, adding that it would provide additional details at a later time.

Frontier Airlines, which overlaps with Spirit on more than 100 routes, plans to "focus on helping people continue their travel plans with low-fare options," a spokeswoman said.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Axia Energia SA expected to post earnings of R$1.25 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Axia Energia SA , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 5 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Rio De Janeiro-based company is expected to report a 17.3% increase in revenue to R$12.216 billion from R$10.41 billion a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Axia Energia SA is for earnings of R$1.25 per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 8 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

data.title: Axia Energia SA expected to post earnings of 29 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Axia Energia SA , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 5 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Rio De Janeiro-based company is expected to report a 27.8% increase in revenue to $2.275 billion from $1.78 billion a year ago, according to the estimate from one analyst, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Axia Energia SA is for earnings of 29 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Axia Energia SA is $13.50, about 7.6% above its last closing price of $12.55

data.title: Airlines Seen Ready to Fill Void Left by Spirit — Market Talk
data.description: If Spirit Airlines does liquidate its business, as The Wall Street Journal reported it is planning to do, other airlines are likely going to jump in to fill the gap it creates, Melius analyst write in a note. "When Spirit filed its second bankruptcy in September 2025, Frontier, JetBlue, and United all made concerted efforts to backfill Spirit's markets out of Florida," the analysts write. "We expect all three to be even more aggressive now given the potential jump ball for assets and routes." But though Frontier and JetBlue saw the biggest stock price gains following the Journal's report, the analysts see Delta and United as benefiting the most from structural changes in the industry, with other airlines needing market conditions to ease before catching up. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

data.title: Treasuries Move Modestly Higher, Extending Yesterday's Rebound
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Treasuries saw modest strength during trading on Friday, extending the rebound seen over the course of the previous session.

Bond prices gave back ground after an early advance but remained in positive territory. As a result, the yield on the benchmark ten-year note, which moves opposite of its price, dipped 1.2 basis points to 4.378 percent.

The ten-year yield added to the 2.8 basis point drop seen on Thursday, pulling back further off Wednesday's one-month closing high.

Treasuries showed a strong move to the upside following the release of a report from the Institute for Supply Management U.S. manufacturing activity continued to expand in April but at a slightly slower than expected pace.

The ISM said its manufacturing PMI came in at 52.7 in April, unchanged from March. While a reading above 50 still indicates growth, economists had expected the index to inch up to 53.0.

The strength among treasuries also came amid an extended pullback by the price of crude oil, with U.S. crude oil futures tumbling by nearly 3 percent.

U.S. crude oil futures showed a significant downturn on Thursday, slumping by 1.7 percent after reaching their highest levels in four years.

The continued decrease by crude oil prices came amid reports Iran has delivered its response to the latest U.S. amendments on the agreement to end the war through Pakistani mediators.

Traders seemed to shrug off the response from President Donald Trump, who told reporters at the White House that he was 'not satisfied' with Iran's proposal.
